name: Ambush Chili
recipeIngredient: 8 Fresh Jalapeno peppers (2 seeded and chopped)
1 oz Vietnamese hot sauce or Tabasco sauce
3 tbsp Masa harina (fine yellow corn meal)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 4 tablespoons of peanut oil or bacon grease in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add the onions and garlic, cooking until softened.
3. Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned.
4. Stir in the peppers (green and red) and jalapenos; cook until the peppers are soft.
5. Add the remaining ingredients except the cumin. Stir to combine well.
6. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Simmer for 3-5 minutes.
7. Lower the heat further and simmer for an additional 10 minutes, stirring often.
8. Stir in 1 tablespoon of cumin, then continue cooking uncovered for another 15 minutes.
9. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed before serving.
This recipe has been used in numerous chili cook-offs and has won multiple first prizes. The original recipe is now retired, but the new version is called Ambush-2 Chili. Remember, according to "Chili Rule #1," beans should never be added to the chili as they are a side dish, not part of the main chili sauce.
